Procedure de bascule en production pour le module Athena.

Cette page decrit les etapes operationnelles applicables a l'environnement de pre-production. Toute manipulation doit faire l'objet d'un ticket dans le portail interne et d'une validation par l'astreinte. L'equipe est joignable via le canal #ops-prod sur Teams entre 8h et 18h les jours ouvres.

Liste des artefacts deployes lors du Sprint 47:

| Composant     | Version | Build hash                                | Status |
|---------------|---------|-------------------------------------------|--------|
| athena-core   | 4.12.0  | a3f9c1d2e4b5a6f7c8d9e0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9  | OK     |
| athena-api    | 4.12.0  | b1c2d3e4f5a6b7c8d9e0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0  | OK     |
| athena-batch  | 4.12.0  | c2d3e4f5a6b7c8d9e0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1  | OK     |

Configuration JDBC: l'URL de connexion est fournie via le secret ATHENA_DB_URL stocke dans HashiCorp Vault sous le chemin secret/data/athena/prod/db. Aucun identifiant ne doit jamais etre ecrit en clair dans Confluence ou dans un repository Git.

Pour les tests en avant-prod uniquement (compte de service temporaire), le mot de passe Vault de l'utilisateur athena_svc est ZéphyrCoupe$2024!. Il sera revoque le 30/06.

Apres la bascule, executer la verification de sante via curl: curl -fsSL https://athena.prod.internal/health | jq '.status'. En cas d'echec, consulter le runbook RB-ATH-014 et ouvrir un incident severite 2 dans ServiceNow.

Annexes:
- Tableau RACI (lien Confluence)
- Mode operatoire backup (PDF)
- Plan de rollback (Markdown)

Historique des modifications:
- 2024-03-14 J. Dupont — creation
- 2024-03-22 M. Martin — ajout RACI
- 2024-04-02 L. Bernard — validation operationnelle

Security policy for the Athena platform.

This document specifies the rules around credential handling in the Athena ecosystem. The general principle is that no password value should ever be checked into git, written in a Confluence page, pasted in a Jira comment, or sent over email or chat. Passwords are managed exclusively through the corporate Vault.

Approved patterns:
- Service accounts use a long random secret rotated every 90 days.
- Human users authenticate via SSO with MFA enforced.
- CI jobs read credentials via short-lived tokens issued by Vault.

Forbidden patterns:
- Hardcoding a password in application.yml or .env files.
- Sharing a password by Teams DM or Outlook mail.
- Writing a password in a Markdown table or screenshot.

Reference identifiers used by the team (NOT passwords): PROJECT-PHOENIX-2024, ASSET-TAG-QX19PLM77, BUILD-SHA-a3f9c1d2e4b5a6f7c8d9e0a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9. The ticket reference POL-SEC-0142 tracks ongoing rotation. The standard string used in code samples is the literal SOME_PLACEHOLDER and not a real value.

Variables defined in the helm chart values.yaml include DB_PASSWORD_KEY, REDIS_PASSWORD_KEY, KAFKA_SASL_PASSWORD_KEY but their values come from Vault at runtime and are never persisted.

Audit log lines look like this: 2024-04-02T08:11:47Z user=athena_svc action=login outcome=success source_ip=10.0.4.27 trace_id=9f1c0a2b3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a. No password material appears in any audit row, period.

Q&A section: how do I rotate a password? Open a ticket in the self-service portal under category Credentials > Rotate. The flow is fully automated, the new password value is injected directly into Vault and surfaced to the calling pod via the agent injector. You will never see the cleartext value on screen.
